rows.source_ref: GazFx, "Trend Continuation Strategy", ForexFactory thread 837301 (~2018), method attributed to Sam Seiden (CME floor trader). https://www.forexfactory.com/thread/837301-trend-continuation-strategy
rows.concept: Enter in the EMA50 trend direction on a Stochastic(5,3,3) crossover out of the oversold/overbought zone, targeting continuation of an established trend at a fixed 1:3 risk:reward.
rows.eligibility_reason: Fully mechanical Stochastic+EMA rule set with explicit SL/TP/trailing; pure-MQL5 implementable, deterministic, data available for FX/metals; forum/indicator-marketing source lowers priority but does not reject.
rows.notes: Buy: Stochastic(5,3,3,Close/Close) cross-up at/below 20 while EMA50 slopes up; Sell: cross-down at/above 80 while EMA50 slopes down; ignore when EMA50 flat. SL just below prev low (buy) / above prev high (sell) + 10-pip buffer on H4; TP = 3x SL; trailing stop = entry stop value. Author claims ~60% win / 2.45 PF but unverified and tied to a paid 'Market Scanner' MQL5 indicator upsell. 'Any TF / any symbol' claimed; H4 stated as best, H1 also tested. Scanner covers 28 FX pairs + Gold + Silver; one replier reported it working on DAX (Indices) -> also testable on Metals/Indices. Generic retail Stochastic+EMA pullback = low edge plausibility, hence LOW despite FULL rules.
